sentence: O tempo não está nada bom hoje de tarde.
ipa: ˈu ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈsta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ˈoʒe ˈde ˈtaɾde
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all this afternoon.
notes: Conservative Gaúcho: final /e/ retained as [e] in 'hoje'/'tarde', not raised to [i] (mateus_dandrade2000); non-palatalising dental /d/ in 'de'/'tarde' per silva2002.

sentence: Estes chinelos custam quarenta reais na feira.
ipa: ˈestes ʃiˈnelos ˈkustɐ̃ kaˈɾẽtɐ reˈajs ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: These sandals cost forty reais at the market.
notes: Alveolar trill onset [r] in 'reais' — the European-sounding Gaúcho rhotic, not the guttural [ʁ] (silva2002); alveolar coda /s/ [s] in 'estes'/'reais', no palatalisation, per the falares do Sul division of nascentes1953.

sentence: A consulta é amanhã às três da tarde.
ipa: ˈɐ kõˈsuwtɐ ˈɛ amaˈɲɐ̃ ˈas ˈtɾes ˈdɐ ˈtaɾde
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three in the afternoon.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /l/ vocalised to [w] in 'consulta' as in general BR (silva2002); alveolar coda /s/ in 'às'/'três' per nascentes1953.

sentence: Meu irmão mora aqui perto do arroio.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmɐ̃w̃ ˈmoɾɐ aˈki ˈpeɾtu ˈdu aˈroju
gloss_en: My brother lives right near the stream.
notes: Regional lexicon 'arroio' (stream) from the Spanish/Platine adstrate of the Gaúcho region, documented for southern BR by noll2008; trill [r] in intervocalic 'arroio' (silva2002); nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'irmão' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Segue reto e dobra na próxima esquina.
ipa: ˈseɡe ˈretu ˈe ˈdobɾɐ ˈnɐ ˈpɾɔʃimɐ eˈskinɐ
gloss_en: Go straight and turn at the next corner.
notes: Alveolar trill onset [r] in 'reto' (silva2002); open-mid [ɔ] in 'próxima' (mateus_dandrade2000); 'reto e' vowel-junction sandhi (callou_leite2001).

sentence: A costela com mandioca estava tri boa.
ipa: ˈɐ koˈstelɐ ˈkõ mɐ̃diˈokɐ eˈstavɐ ˈtɾi ˈboɐ
gloss_en: The rib with cassava was really good.
notes: Gaúcho intensifier 'tri' (= 'muito') and churrasco lexicon ('costela'), southern-BR register documented by noll2008; nasal [õ] in 'com', dental /d/ in 'mandioca' (silva2002).

sentence: Onde é que tu deixaste o chimarrão?
ipa: ˈõde ˈɛ ˈke ˈtu deˈjʃaste ˈu ʃimaˈrɐ̃w̃
gloss_en: Where did you leave the mate?
notes: Second-person 'tu' with 2sg preterite 'deixaste' — the Gaúcho campeiro paradigm attested for RS by cardoso_etal2014; 'chimarrão' (mate) regional lexicon (noll2008); trill [r] and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'chimarrão' (silva2002).

sentence: Quantos cavalos tem na tua estância?
ipa: ˈkɐ̃tos kaˈvalos ˈtẽ ˈnɐ ˈtuɐ eˈstɐ̃s̺iɐ
gloss_en: How many horses are there on your ranch?
notes: 'tua' + Platine ranching lexicon 'estância', southern-BR register (noll2008); possessive 'tua' consistent with the 'tu' paradigm of cardoso_etal2014; 'tua estância' vowel-junction sandhi (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Se tu quisesses, comprava aquela chácara.
ipa: ˈse ˈtu kiˈzeses kõˈpɾavɐ aˈkelɐ ˈʃakaɾɐ
gloss_en: If you wanted, I would buy that little farm.
notes: 'tu' + 2sg imperfect subjunctive 'quisesses' (cardoso_etal2014); reduced-apodosis conditional with imperfect 'comprava' typical of colloquial BR (noll2008); dental /d/ retained (silva2002).

sentence: Tu compraste as frutas e as verduras na venda?
ipa: ˈtu kõˈpɾaste ˈas ˈfɾutas ˈe ˈas veˈɾduɾas ˈnɐ ˈvẽdɐ
gloss_en: Did you buy the fruit and the vegetables at the store?
notes: 'tu' + 2sg 'compraste' (cardoso_etal2014); 'venda' (small country store) regional lexicon (noll2008); alveolar coda /s/ across 'as frutas'/'as verduras', no palatalisation (nascentes1953).

sentence: Amanhã nós vamos de carro até a serra.
ipa: amaˈɲɐ̃ ˈnɔs ˈvamos ˈde ˈkaru aˈtɛ ˈɐ ˈserɐ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we are going by car to the highlands.
notes: Trill [r] in 'carro'/'serra' (silva2002); palatal [ɲ] and nasal in 'amanhã' (mateus_dandrade2000); open-mid [ɔ] in 'nós' (silva2002).

sentence: Me liga quando tu chegares em casa, tchê.
ipa: ˈme ˈliɡɐ ˈkɐ̃du ˈtu ʃeˈɡaɾes ˈẽ ˈkazɐ ˈtʃe
gloss_en: Call me when you get home, mate.
notes: 'tu' + 2sg future subjunctive 'chegares' (cardoso_etal2014); Gaúcho vocative 'tchê' [tʃe] from the Platine adstrate (noll2008); nasal [ẽ] in 'em' (silva2002).

sentence: Bah, buenas, como é que tu vais, tchê?
ipa: ˈba buˈenas ˈkomu ˈɛ ˈke ˈtu ˈvajs ˈtʃe
gloss_en: Wow, hey, how are you doing, mate?
notes: Gaúcho discourse markers 'bah', 'buenas' and 'tchê' — Spanish/Platine adstrate greetings characteristic of RS speech (noll2008); 'tu vais' 2sg (cardoso_etal2014); 'como é' vowel-junction sandhi (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois voando.
ipa: ˈmajs ˈvale ˈũ ˈpasaɾu ˈnɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ˈke ˈdojs voˈɐ̃du
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Proverb in neutral register; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'mão' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /s/ [s] in 'mais'/'dois' with no palatalisation (nascentes1953).

sentence: O carro do Rodrigo ferrou na estrada de terra.
ipa: ˈu ˈkaru ˈdu roˈdɾiɡu feˈrow ˈnɐ eˈstɾadɐ ˈde ˈterɐ
gloss_en: Rodrigo's car broke down on the dirt road.
notes: Dense alveolar trill [r] in 'carro'/'Rodrigo'/'ferrou'/'terra' — the defining Gaúcho onset/rr rhotic (silva2002; mateus_dandrade2000); [ow] in 'ferrou' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: O leiteiro trouxe pouco leite outro dia.
ipa: ˈu leˈjtejɾu ˈtɾowʃe ˈpowku ˈlejte ˈowtɾu ˈdiɐ
gloss_en: The milkman brought little milk the other day.
notes: Retained falling diphthongs [ej]/[ow] in 'leiteiro'/'pouco'/'outro' (mateus_dandrade2000); final /e/ kept as [e] in 'leite' (silva2002).

sentence: O sol forte queimava o milharal.
ipa: ˈu ˈsow ˈfoɾte keˈjmavɐ ˈu miʎaˈɾaw
gloss_en: The strong sun scorched the cornfield.
notes: Palatal [ʎ] in 'milharal' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /l/ vocalised to [w] in 'sol'/'milharal' as in general BR (silva2002); [ej] in 'queimava' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: A filha da vizinha mexia no ninho com galho.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈfiʎɐ ˈdɐ viˈziɲɐ meˈʃiɐ ˈnu ˈniɲu ˈkõ ˈɡaʎu
gloss_en: The neighbour's daughter poked at the nest with a twig.
notes: Palatals [ʎ] ('filha','galho') and [ɲ] ('vizinha','ninho') (mateus_dandrade2000); dental /d/ in 'da' (silva2002).

sentence: O café estava ótimo, mas a torta ficou meio seca.
ipa: ˈu kaˈfɛ eˈstavɐ ˈɔtimu ˈmas ˈɐ ˈtoɾtɐ fiˈkow ˈmeju ˈsekɐ
gloss_en: The coffee was great, but the pie came out a bit dry.
notes: Open-mids [ɛ] ('café') and [ɔ] ('ótimo') (mateus_dandrade2000); [ow] in 'ficou', [ej] in 'meio' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Quem não tem cão caça com gato, dizia a velha.
ipa: ˈkẽ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ˈtẽ ˈkɐ̃w̃ ˈkasɐ ˈkõ ˈɡatu diˈziɐ ˈɐ ˈveʎɐ
gloss_en: Who has no dog hunts with a cat, the old woman used to say.
notes: Nasal diphthongs [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não'/'cão' (mateus_dandrade2000); palatal [ʎ] in 'velha' (silva2002); 'dizia a' vowel-junction sandhi (callou_leite2001).
